title: Jayavarman VII : Roi et Bâtisseur du Cambodge
highlights:
Bouddhisme et pouvoir royal Couronné en 1182, Jayavarman VII règne sur un territoire plus vaste et plus administré que ses prédécesseurs. Son règne marque une rupture spirituelle majeure : il introduit le bouddhisme Mahayana comme fondement du pouvoir politique et religieux. Dans cette perspective, le roi est vu comme un bodhisattva, un être éveillé au service de son peuple. Le royaume reste très hiérarchisé : le roi demeure distant, invisible, reclus dans son palais.  ...    Le Bayon, temple d’État au cœur d’Angkor Thom, célèbre pour ses visages monumentaux.

Le Preah Khan, dédié à son père. Le Ta Prohm, dédié à sa mère.

title: Heritage Line Magazine I King Jayavarman VII – Hero of the Khmer ...
highlights: warrior, a visionary, and a  ...    Jayavarman VII is best remembered for his ambitious building projects, the most famous being Angkor Thom, a vast city complex that became the heart of his empire. Its grand gates, massive stone walls, and intricate carvings reflected  ...    its enigmatic smiling stone faces, believed to represent either the king himself or the Bodhisattva of compassion. Unlike his predecessors, who were predominantly Hindu, Jayavarman VII was a devout Mahayana Buddhist. His religious beliefs deeply influenced his rule, leading to the construction of numerous Buddhist temples and shrines throughout the  ...    More than just a warrior and builder, Jayavarman VII was a ruler with a profound sense of social responsibility. He established an extensive network of hospitals, rest houses, and roads, ensuring that his people had access to medical  ...    • He was the first Khmer king to prioritize Buddhism over Hinduism, reshaping the spiritual landscape of the empire.•

title: Jayavarman VII - Wikipedia
highlights:
He was the first Khmer king devoted to Buddhism; only one other had been a Buddhist. He built the Bayon as a monument to Buddhism. Historians generally consider Jayavarman VII the most powerful of the Khmer monarchs. His government built many projects, including hospitals, highways, rest houses, and temples. With Buddhism as his motivation, Jayavarman VII is credited with introducing a welfare state that served the Khmer people's physical and spiritual needs.  ...    | Mother | Sri Jayarajacudamani |
